description:
Goal
Make compiler2 semantic analysis terminate cleanly on recursive list/higher-order fixtures by fixing the contract-matching / activation-analysis model in the right place, then re-enable the disabled regression tests.

Problem being fixed
This ticket no longer covers only Enum.with_index/2. We now have two concrete repro families in the same semantic seam:
- Enum.with_index/2 with a mapper closure times out in semantic analysis with repeated AnalyzeActivation retractions/redefinitions through the Enum/List reduce SCC.
- quicksort native/backend proof tests can stack-overflow in semantic analysis before transport/native lowering begins. LLDB traces the overflow into src/compiler2/contract.rs:collect_ambiguous_empty_list_vars during function-contract application while analyzing the rooted recursive quicksort frontier.

This is not a runtime-demand/native transport bug. When it fires, it blocks the current ownership-boundary arc because quicksort is one of the acceptance fixtures for fz-hwn.10/fz-hwn.6. So it must move in front of them instead of staying deferred behind cleanup.

Concrete repros
- cargo test compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts --lib
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_keeps_only_the_closed_quicksort_inventory --lib -- --nocapture
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_matches_tuple_field_call_continuations_to_the_callee_return_abi --lib -- --nocapture
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_quicksort_through_compiler2_codegen --lib

Observed facts
- --log-telemetry for Enum.with_index shows activation_analysis / return_type / callsite churn in the Enum.to_list -> Enum.reverse -> Enum.reduce -> Enumerable(List).reduce -> List.reduce_cont/reduce_step SCC.
- LLDB for quicksort shows stack overflow inside collect_ambiguous_empty_list_vars, reached from semantic::analyze_activation while applying function contracts.
- The quicksort failure appears before native transport/codegen meaningfully begin, so it should not be worked around in runtime-demand or native.

Required work
1. Reproduce the semantic failure at the smallest level that still exposes the bug, ideally with contract/unit coverage in addition to fixture coverage.
2. Fix the recursive contract-matching / semantic-analysis model in the right place. In particular, do not add timeouts, depth caps, or fixture-specific special cases.
3. Make recursive type/contract traversal terminate structurally on cyclic or repeated type pairs while preserving the existing empty-list underconstraint intent.
4. Re-enable compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts and make the quicksort native/backend proof fixtures pass for the semantic reason, not because they were ignored.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/contract.rs
- src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs
- src/compiler2/types/* as needed for structurally memoized recursive comparisons/traversals

Checks
- cargo test compiler2_interp_runs_enum_with_index_mapper_from_backend_artifacts --lib
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_keeps_only_the_closed_quicksort_inventory --lib -- --nocapture
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_matches_tuple_field_call_continuations_to_the_callee_return_abi --lib -- --nocapture
- cargo test compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_quicksort_through_compiler2_codegen --lib
- target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry <path> interp <Enum.with_index repro> completes without drive timeout
- quicksort semantic/native proof fixtures complete without stack overflow
title: Compiler2 semantic analysis: contract matching recurses or churns on higher-order recursive fixtures
